examples: With a little help from this carbonic acid, moving water forms most of the world's limestone caves.
Also, in surface water, there is a weak acid, carbonic acid, not sulfuric acid, but carbonic acid that helps dissolve the rock.
Once in the water, it reacts to form carbonic acid.
In sea water, this forms carbonic acid which adds to ocean acidification, with consequent problems for biodiversity.
But as CO2 dissolves in seawater carbonic acid is formed, causing the ocean to acidify at rates not seen for the last 20 million years.
